title: XXE Vulnerability Exploitation Attempt Leading to DoS

steps.message: Block external entity references within the WAF/firewall, specifically targeting patterns like `<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM ...>` to prevent resource fetching across the network and potential denial of service via indefinite file stream reads (`file:///dev/random`), as described in the KB.

Initiate an automated vulnerability scan of the affected application (e.g., mdsec.net/bank/27/Default.aspx) to identify all instances where user-supplied XML data is parsed without proper validation or disabling external entities, which could lead to attacks like XXE or SOAP message manipulation.

Query SIEM logs for historical and ongoing attempts to exploit XXE vulnerabilities, specifically looking for patterns like `SYSTEM "file:///"`, `SYSTEM "http://192.168.1.1:25"`, or indications of large indefinite file stream reads from `/dev/random`, as these signal attempts to interact with the filesystem or backend systems as mentioned in the KB.

Obtain approval to deploy an emergency patch that disables external entity processing (e.g., `setFeature(XMLConstants.FEATURE_SECURE_PROCESSING, true)`) and ensures proper input validation for XML payloads in the affected application to prevent XXE and SOAP injection vulnerabilities that interfere with application logic.

Communicate the containment of the XXE-induced DoS incident and the scheduled patch deployment via Slack, and create a follow-up ticket in Jira for long-term remediation and a comprehensive review of XML parsing mechanisms.

params.message: HIGH: XXE vulnerability exploitation leading to DoS contained. Patch deployment approved. Opening follow-up ticket for full remediation.
params.summary: Full remediation of XXE/SOAP vulnerabilities in application
